metadata.title: Build a Quantum Energy Ladder with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent.

metadata.audio_note: All existing inner-beat mp3s must be regenerated with kokoro af_kore (existing use voice_id 1sgY6Voq1aexKOB1IJ2D directly): python3 runtime/scripts/generate_audio.py quantum-mechanics-vol1/youtube/medhavy-vol1-pib-spectrum
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: One prompt into Claude Code. One Python file appears. Run it and the particle-in-a-box energy ladder draws itself — E_n proportional to n-squared, the floor that can never be zero. Watch quantum mechanics build itself.

props.segment: Build a Quantum Energy Ladder with Claude Code + Manim
props.command: claude "build a particle-in-a-box energy ladder with Manim"

beats.narration_text: The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, infinite square well, L equals two nanometers, electron mass. Draw the first three energy levels as horizontal lines with sine-wave eigenfunctions. Label each level with its energy in electron-volts. Three things in every prompt: the rule, the numbers, what to render. Under-specify and the generated scene needs a re-do.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  infinite square well, L = 2 nm, m = mₑ
  draw energy levels n=1,2,3 with
  sine eigenstates at each rung
  label E_n in eV"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running — read it. Find this line: n-squared times E-one. That is the entire physics. The n-squared exponent is where to look. Check that E-one is twelve-forty squared times pi-squared over twice the mass, times L-squared — in electron-volts. Check n equals one gives point-zero-nine-four. Thirty seconds. Not optional.

props.code:
# pib_spectrum.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
L = 2e-9          # m  well width
hbar = 1.0546e-34 # J·s
m_e  = 9.109e-31  # kg

E1 = (np.pi**2 * hbar**2) / (2 * m_e * L**2)
E1_eV = E1 / 1.602e-19   # ≈ 0.094 eV

def E_n(n):          # ← the physics: n²·E₁
    return n**2 * E1_eV

# n=1: 0.094 eV   n=2: 0.376 eV   n=3: 0.846 eV
# ratio E₂/E₁ = 4.000  (n²)  ✓

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: the ratio E-two over E-one. The ratio chip shows four-point-zero-zero-zero. That is the n-squared law — exact, not approximate. Second: E-one is above zero. The ground state rests at point-zero-nine-four electron-volts. No matter how you cool this system the particle never stops. Both correct. Ready to iterate.

Iterate: claude — update pib-spectrum dot py — add a parameter L that the user can change. Show that when L halves from two to one nanometer, E-one quadruples from point-zero-nine-four to point-three-seven-six electron-volts. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The physics was the excuse to run the loop. That is what you carry forward.

props.command:
claude "update pib_spectrum.py:
  → add parameter L (default 2 nm)
  → show E₁ for L=2 nm and L=1 nm
  → label: E₁ ∝ 1/L² (halve L → 4×E₁)"

beats.narration_text: The ladder is yours now. Paste this into Claude and push the well to finite depth — what happens to the ground state energy?

props.segment: Build a Quantum Energy Ladder with Claude Code + Manim
props.command: claude "extend the particle-in-a-box simulation: add a finite well, plot how the wavefunctions leak outside, compare bound state energies to the infinite case"

beats.narration_text: If this made the energy ladder click, subscribe. Medhavy.

props.title: Build a Quantum Energy Ladder with Claude Code + Manim.
